title: CLI can't create or rename projects — add add-project and rename-project commands

brief.why: Had to use the UI for basic project management during GTD context restructure

brief.done: Can create and rename projects from the CLI without touching the UI

title: Update all project name references from @ prefix to At prefix

brief.why: Projects were renamed (At Work, At Comms, At Review, At Home, At Claude, At Timed, Waiting For). Skill documentation and CLI examples still reference @Work, @Wait, @Ping etc. Every future Claude session loading this skill will query wrong names and get 0 results.
brief.what: Update SKILL.md, references/*.md, and any CLI examples to use new project names. Update the team member filtering examples.
brief.done: Skill loads correctly with current Todoist project names. All query examples return expected results.

done_note: Subsumed by tgt-nujeni (skill rewrite). The @ to At rename is part of the larger skill rewrite for multi-user support.

brief.why: Production since Jan 2026 but field reports surface rough edges
brief.what: Section validation, PATH handling, Sublime Loop docs, timeout verification
brief.done: Weekly review workflow runs without workarounds

title: Update token_store.py to use plugin data dir on Linux

brief.why: ~/.todoist-token is a loose file; plugin data dir is version-stable and consistent with mise
brief.what: 1. Add _PLUGIN_DATA_DIR constant (~/.claude/plugins/data/todoist-gtd-batterie-de-savoir) 2. Change TOKEN_FILE fallback to data dir / token 3. Add legacy migration from ~/.todoist-token on first read
brief.done: On Linux: token at ~/.claude/plugins/data/todoist-gtd-batterie-de-savoir/token, ~/.todoist-token migrated transparently, tests pass

steps: Change TOKEN_FILE fallback to data dir / token
Add legacy migration from ~/.todoist-token on first read

title: Team members' Claudes see their own tasks without manual filtering

brief.why: Shared projects return 70+ tasks for all team members. Claudes dump everything and users must manually ask to filter by assignee. The CLI has no concept of 'who am I' — assignee_id is a raw numeric string with no name resolution.
brief.what: 1. Add get_current_user() using REST v1 /user endpoint and todoist whoami command 2. Enrich task output with resolved assignee names 3. Default to current user's tasks on shared projects, add --team flag to widen
brief.done: todoist whoami returns current user. todoist tasks --project 'Desired Outcomes Q2' returns only my tasks with assignee names visible. --team returns all. No hardcoded user IDs anywhere.

done_note: whoami command, assignee name enrichment, auto-filter to current user on shared projects with --team to widen. Caught and fixed edge case where personal projects with collaborators excluded unassigned tasks.

title: Split SKILL.md to reduce line count

brief.what: Move detailed sections (Data Model, CLI Query Patterns) to references/

title: Add get_current_user() and todoist whoami command

brief.why: CLI has no concept of identity — can't tell whose tasks are whose without manually cross-referencing collaborator lists
brief.what: 1. Add get_current_user(token) to common.py calling GET /api/v1/user 2. Add cmd_whoami to cli.py outputting id, full_name, email 3. Wire up argparse subcommand
brief.done: todoist whoami prints current user's name, email, and ID. Function reusable by other commands.

steps: Add get_current_user(token) to common.py calling GET /api/v1/user
Add cmd_whoami to cli.py outputting id, full_name, email

title: comment_count guard silently hides comments with attachments

brief.why: task and tasks commands check comment_count before fetching comments, but Todoist API returns 0 even when file-attachment comments exist. User sees attachments in UI but CLI reports empty comments array.
brief.what: 1. Remove comment_count guard from cmd_get_task (cli.py:170) 2. Remove comment_count guard from cmd_get_tasks (cli.py:142) 3. Always fetch comments or use a try-with-fallback 4. Add test case with file attachment comment
brief.done: todoist task ID returns the comment with attachment for the 'Review with claude' task (6gGMmvgrffvRMG7p)

title: Skill coaches any team member's Claude on GTD without hardcoded personal setup

brief.why: SKILL.md hardcodes Sameer's project names, MIT concordance table, and personal GTD layout. Team members loading the skill get wrong project names and misleading coaching. Also, GTD methodology is shallow — needs NAA depth (horizons of focus, natural planning model, 5 steps).
brief.what: 1. Remove hardcoded project names and concordance table 2. Make skill reference dynamic CLI features (whoami, --team) 3. Enrich GTD methodology from Next Action Associates framework 4. Update all @ prefix references to current project names 5. Test with skill-forge evals against neutral Claudes
brief.done: Neutral Claude loading the skill correctly uses identity-aware CLI features, follows GTD methodology, and doesn't reference any hardcoded user-specific setup. Evals pass.

title: Split SKILL.md and fix lint warnings

brief.why: SKILL.md is 565 lines (max 500). Lint flags line count, missing When NOT to Use section, and extra frontmatter field. Data Model and CLI Query Patterns are reference material that doesn't need to be in the main skill file.
brief.what: 1. Create references/CLI_REFERENCE.md with CLI Setup, Query Patterns, and Data Model sections 2. Replace extracted sections in SKILL.md with brief summaries and links 3. Fix lint warnings (frontmatter requires field, proper When NOT to Use section) 4. Run lint and CSO, verify under 500 lines
brief.done: SKILL.md under 500 lines, lint passes with no line_count warning, CSO score maintained or improved

title: Strip OAuth dead code from todoist auth and add --token flag

brief.why: OAuth flow is 400 lines that never work (PLACEHOLDER credentials). Meanwhile Claude can't call todoist auth non-interactively because it uses input(). Both block smooth first-run setup.
brief.what: Remove OAuth machinery from auth.py (keep _personal_token_flow). Add --token TOKEN flag for non-interactive use. Update SKILL.md to use --token path. Remove --manual/--code flags.
brief.done: todoist auth works interactively (opens URL, prompts). todoist auth --token TOKEN works non-interactively. OAuth code deleted. SKILL.md updated.

done_note: Stripped OAuth dead code (400 lines incl. PLACEHOLDER credentials, HTTP server, callback handler, manual flow, HTML templates). Added --token flag for non-interactive token storage. No-flags path prints setup instructions.

title: Default to current user's tasks on shared projects

brief.why: Every Claude session on shared projects dumps 70+ tasks then has to be told to filter — the default should be 'my tasks' with --team to see everyone's
brief.what: 1. Detect shared projects (has collaborators) 2. Auto-filter to current user's assignee_id by default 3. Add --team flag to show all tasks 4. Update help text
brief.done: todoist tasks --project 'Desired Outcomes Q2' returns only current user's tasks. todoist tasks --project 'Desired Outcomes Q2' --team returns all 70+. Personal projects unaffected.

steps: Auto-filter to current user's assignee_id by default
Add --team flag to show all tasks

brief.what: Check section/project exists before API call, show available options on error

title: Enrich task output with resolved assignee names

brief.why: assignee_id is a raw numeric string — Claudes and humans can't tell whose task it is without a separate collaborator lookup
brief.what: 1. Fetch collaborators for the task's project 2. Resolve assignee_id to name in to_dict output 3. Add assignee_name field to JSON output
brief.done: todoist tasks --project 'Desired Outcomes Q2' shows assignee_name on each task. Unassigned tasks show null.

steps: Fetch collaborators for the task's project
Resolve assignee_id to name in to_dict output

title: Token stored in plugin data dir on Linux

brief.why: On Linux, Keychain is unavailable so token falls back to ~/.todoist-token — a loose file in home with no stable relationship to the plugin. mise-batterie-de-savoir uses ~/.claude/plugins/data/ for the same problem and survives plugin upgrades.
brief.what: 1. Add _PLUGIN_DATA_DIR constant pointing to ~/.claude/plugins/data/todoist-gtd-batterie-de-savoir 2. Change TOKEN_FILE fallback from ~/.todoist-token to plugin data dir / token 3. Add legacy migration: if ~/.todoist-token exists and data dir is empty, copy forward on first read
brief.done: On Linux: token stored at ~/.claude/plugins/data/todoist-gtd-batterie-de-savoir/token, existing ~/.todoist-token migrated transparently, tests pass

done_note: Done this session — stored token in plugin data dir on kube
